title: Notational Intelligence, Linus Lee | Compile 26
summary: Linus Lee discusses notational intelligence, the idea that the ways we write down ideas shape how we think about them. He explores how deep learning could help invent entirely new mathematical and symbolic notations that might reveal new structures in reasoning and communication.
writeup: Linus Lee presents notational intelligence: the premise that the systems used to write ideas down shape how those ideas are thought through. The talk argues that notation is not neutral, since the form of symbolic representation influences reasoning and communication. The central proposal is that deep learning could be used to design entirely new mathematical and symbolic notations, potentially surfacing structures in reasoning that existing notations obscure.
